calendar.matters:
Motion For Equitable Estoppel
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL

Appearances:
Patricia Ruiz Calzada of the Law Office of Patricia Ruiz Calzada, Inc. (925.237.1403) appearing via Zoom for defendant Bryant Law Group, LLP; No other appearances.

This matter is called at 9:51 am and there is no appearance by plaintiff, who contests the tentative ruling. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for equitable estoppel by plaintiff Gary M. Waite is denied. It appears Waite is seeking judgment or partial judgment, and presents evidence in support, but he has not satisfied the procedural requirements for a summary judgment motion, which must be brought pursuant to the requirements set out in Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 437c. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Continued To Jul-14-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Quash Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-01-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Gary M. White, 775-990-2097 garym.waite78@gmail.com Plaintiff In Pro Per

No appearance by Defendant Edward M. Higginbotham

Matter was not contested by Plaintiff appears our of an abundance of caution.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM's Motion To Quash Second Amended Complaint.

Specially appearing defendant Edward Higginbotham's motion to quash is denied as moot. No proof of service of the summons as to Higginbotham has been filed, and the court rejected plaintiff Gary Waite's request to take his default. For clarity, the court explains that each party in an action is entitled to service of the summons and complaint. Waite's service of the summons and complaint on the Bryant Law Group, LLP, did not effect service on Higginbotham individually because attorneys for Bryant Law Group have not appeared in this action as counsel for Higginbotham and every party to an action has a right to individual service pursuant to the Code unless they agree to some other method of service. Waite must serve Higginbotham as required by the Code in order for the complaint to be at issue as to Higginbotham.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEMURRER OF BRYANT LAW GROUP, LLP TO GARY M. WAITE'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances via Zoom:
Gary M. Waite (415.877.0496, garym.waite78@gmail.com) plaintiff and opposing party appearing in propria persona;
Ian Kelly of the Law Office of Patricia Ruiz Calzada, Inc. (925.237.1403) for defendant and moving party.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

The court exercises its discretion and hears this untimely demurrer. (CCP 430.40(a) [party "may" file a demurrer within 30 days of service]; McAllister v. County of Monterey (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 253, 281-282 [noting trial court's discretion to hear untimely demurrer].

The court sustains the demurrer for uncertainty. (CCP 430.10(f).) Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead causes of action for attorney malpractice, breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duty, and fraud. The third amended complaint must allege "[a] statement of the facts... in ordinary and concise language." (CCP section 425.10(a)(1).) Plaintiff currently alleges repetitive and incomplete claims. For example, plaintiff alleges causes of action for "Duty of Care," "Causation," and "Damages." Those are the elements of a cause of action for negligence, not independent causes of action. (See Thomas v. Stenberg (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 654, 662.) Plaintiff shall remove the redundancies and unnecessary caselaw citations. The third amended complaint shall comply with CRC 2.112 and each cause of action shall identify against whom it is alleged.

Order submitted is under review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
